popularStory.headline: History of Walter de Burgh (1230-1271)
popularStory.description:
Walter de Burgh, 1st Earl of Ulster, 2nd Lord of Connaught (c. 1230 – 28 July 1271), also spelt Bourke or Burke, was an Irish peer from the House of Burke.

Biography

De Burgh was the second son of R …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster於1230年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 高威高威時，他的父親Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught37歲，母親Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught30歲。他於大約1254年與Aveline FitzJohn在英國素瑞結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1271年7月28日在他的家鄉享年41歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 本 馮 克羅斯 本 迪拉利阿塔瑟拉比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster于1230年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 高威高威时，他的父亲Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught37岁，母亲Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught30岁。他于大约1254年与Aveline FitzJohn在英国素瑞结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1271年7月28日在他的家乡享年41岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 本 馮 克羅斯 本 迪拉利阿塔瑟拉比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster was born in 1230, in Galway, County Galway, Ireland, his father, Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught, was 37 and his mother, Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught, was 30. He married Aveline FitzJohn about 1254, in Surrey, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 28 July 1271, in his hometown, at the age of 41, and was buried in Athasselabbey, County of the Cross of Tipperary, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster est né en 1230, à Galway, County Galway, Irlande, son père, Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Aveline FitzJohn environ 1254, à Surrey, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 28 juillet 1271, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterré à Athasselabbey, County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irlande.

Er hat um 1254 in Surrey, England Aveline FitzJohn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 28. Juli 1271 im Alter von 41 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Athasselabbey, County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irland begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulsterが1230年にアイルランドコウントイ ガルワイガルワイで生まれた時，父Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaughtは37歳，母Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaughtは30歳であった。約1254年にイギリススリーでAveline FitzJohnと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1271年7月28日に故郷で41歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ オフ ス クロス オフ ティップエラリーアサセラビーにて埋葬された。

ko.Athassel Abbey, County of the Cross of T: 아일랜드 카운티 프 테 크로스 프 티페라리 앳하셀압브이
ko.lifeSummary: Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster(이)가 1230년 아일랜드 카운티 갈웨이 갈웨이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught(은)는 30세였다. 그는 대략 1254년 영국 수리에서 Aveline FitzJohn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1271년 7월 28일 그의 고향에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 프 테 크로스 프 티페라리 앳하셀압브이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster родился в 1230 г. в местности галвеи, коунти галвеи, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Aveline FitzJohn около 1254 г. в местности сурри, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 28 июля 1271 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 41 год и был похоронен в местности атасселабби, коунти оф т кросс оф типпирэри, Ирландия.
